title: paspalum distichum subsp. paspalodes
intro: Paspalum distichum subsp. paspalodes is a type of grass commonly known as Bahia grass, a tropical grass native to the Americas and the Caribbean. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bahia grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Bahia grass are typically made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
